steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The phrase “that few authors would turn down” is a relative clause immediately following the noun “award”. It modifies “award” directly.

steps.title: Apply the relative clause punctuation rule
steps.content: A relative clause introduced by “that” attaches directly to its noun without intervening punctuation. Choices A (colon), C (comma), and D (semicolon) all insert punctuation between the noun and its modifier. That matches choice B — B is correct.
